a: Improve Rosetta performance by adding support for the total store ordering (TSO) memory model to your Linux kernel.

t: Adding the Virtualization Entitlement to Your Project
a: Configure your project to use the Virtualization framework.
Configure audio devices that enable the guest operating system to perform audio playback and capture through the host’s audio devices.
Share the pasteboard between the host and guest system.
Configure a device that manages multiport console communication with the guest system.

t: Creating and Running a Linux Virtual Machine
a: Design and run custom Linux guests on Apple silicon or Intel-based Mac Computers.
Create custom Virtio drivers for Linux virtual machines.
Configure a device for a guest to display its UI.

t: Installing macOS on a Virtual Machine
a: Download a macOS restore image and install it in a new VM.
Configure devices that connect a mouse and keyboard to the guest system.
Configure a memory balloon device to change the allocated memory for the guest system.
Configure the devices that connect the guest system to the network.
Configure a device for the guest system to use to generate random numbers.

t: Running GUI Linux in a virtual machine on a Mac
a: Install and run GUI Linux in a virtual machine using the Virtualization framework.

t: Running Intel Binaries in Linux VMs
a: Run x86_64 Linux binaries under ARM Linux on Apple silicon.

t: Running Linux in a Virtual Machine
a: Run a Linux operating system on your Mac using the Virtualization framework.

t: Running macOS in a virtual machine on Apple silicon
a: Install and run macOS in a virtual machine using the Virtualization framework.
Configure the serial devices that you use to communicate with the guest system.
Configure devices that share directories from the host into the guest system.
Configure a device that manages port-based communication with the guest system.
Configure the block-storage devices that represent the disks of the guest system.

t: Using iCloud with macOS virtual machines
a: Access iCloud from macOS guest virtual machines.
